From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian long-distance runner

Pavel Loskutov

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | | |
| Medal record | | |
| Men's [athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") | | |
| Representing  [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") | | |
| [European Championships](/wiki/European_Athletics_Championships "European Athletics Championships") | | |
| Silver medal – second place | [2002 Munich](/wiki/2002_European_Athletics_Championships "2002 European Athletics Championships") | [Marathon](/wiki/2002_European_Athletics_Championships_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_marathon "2002 European Athletics Championships – Men's marathon") |

**Pavel Loskutov** (born 2 December 1969 in [Valka](/wiki/Valka "Valka")) is an [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") former long-distance runner who specialized in [marathon races](/wiki/Marathon_race "Marathon race"). He has competed in the Olympic marathon race four times consecutively, from the [1996 Atlanta Olympics](/wiki/1996_Atlanta_Olympics "1996 Atlanta Olympics") to the [2008 Beijing Games](/wiki/2008_Beijing_Games "2008 Beijing Games").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pavel_Loskutov&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

In 2001, he won the [Göteborgsvarvet](/wiki/G%C3%B6teborgsvarvet "Göteborgsvarvet") [half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon") in [Gothenburg](/wiki/Gothenburg "Gothenburg") with a time of 1:03:00. Loskutov finished as the runner-up of the [Paris Marathon](/wiki/Paris_Marathon "Paris Marathon") in 2002 and went on to win a [silver medal](/wiki/Silver_medal "Silver medal") later that year at the [2002 European Championships](/wiki/2002_European_Championships_in_Athletics "2002 European Championships in Athletics") in 2:13:18 hours.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He was the winner of the [JoongAng Seoul Marathon](/wiki/JoongAng_Seoul_Marathon "JoongAng Seoul Marathon") in 2003 and 2004. He also has won half-marathon at the 2008 [Riga Marathon](/wiki/Riga_Marathon "Riga Marathon"). He retired from competition in 2010.
